title: Thelypteris erwinii
taxonomy: Thelypteris erwinii is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae. This species is part of the genus Thelypteris, which belongs to the division Pteridophyta. The classification of Thelypteris erwinii is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae. It is a species of fern, specifically a member of the order Blechnales. Thelypteris erwinii's classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other plants.

intro: Thelypteris erwinii, also known as Erwin's maiden fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may contribute to the treatment of various diseases, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris erwinii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Thelypteris erwinii is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
